_description: ESPN team/player stats for Mexico (3 group + R32) and England (3 group + R32), R16 MEX vs ENG 2026-07-05/06

notes: 3 clean sheets in 3 group games, won all 4 matches this tournament (2W group before Czechia, +R32). Structurally the LOWEST-CORNER team in the tournament (1.67/game average) -- this is a durable team trait, not variance.
Raúl Jiménez: scored vs RSA (1G,2SOT,4sh) and vs Ecuador R32 (1G,1SOT,4sh); blanked vs Korea (0G,1SOT,2sh); rested entirely vs Czechia. 2 goals in 3 appearances (67% scoring rate when featured).

Julián Quiñones: goals in 3 of 4 games (RSA, CZE, ECU), and CRITICALLY has recorded at least 1 shot on target in literally EVERY game this tournament (2,1,1,1 SOT) -- a clean personal floor, not a team-level ceiling projection.

PRIOR LESSON: Mexico's own SOT ceiling logic (never hit 6 SOT in any group game) correctly won +23.44 RBP on MEX-ECU by pricing BELOW a generous crowd -- validated downward-suppression, the safe direction per WINNING_PATTERNS_SYNTHESIS.md.
PRIOR MISS: Mexico corners keep being mispriced -- lost -11.80 on 'MEX-ECU 9+ corners' by trusting a market that undershot despite Mexico's own low-corner history; watch for this again today.
Corners are the single most reliable England structural trait: 8, 9, 7 across all 3 group games -- remarkably consistent regardless of opponent.
Harry Kane: 4 goals in 4 games (75% scoring rate), blanked only vs Ghana. SOT log: 3,1,2,3 (avg 2.25).

Jude Bellingham: 2 goals in 4 games. SOT log: 2,0,1,3 -- hit 2+ SOT in exactly 2 of 4 games: the HIGH-scoring Croatia opener AND the deep-block Congo DR R32 game. The one blank was specifically vs Ghana.

NAMED LESSON (GHA-analogy overfit, confirmed from the England postmortem): twice priced England attacking props DOWN by treating the 0-0 Ghana deep-block performance as the template for how England plays vs defensive opposition, rather than as an outlier/floor. Lost Bellingham 2+SOT (-26.27) and England 7+SOT (-11.36) as a direct result -- England's actual attacking floor vs defensive sides is higher than the Ghana game suggested (they still got 6-7 SOT even vs the deep-block Congo DR side in the very next defensive test). Mexico is a similarly compact, low-corner, defensively organized side -- EXACTLY the profile that could tempt the same overfit again. Correction applied: anchor to the average of all 4 games, not the single worst-case Ghana result.

Submission-discipline note: the single costliest England result (-44.54) was a straight transcription error (Kane 2H SOT), not a modeling error -- read back Kane/Bellingham numbers carefully before submitting today.
